methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RMFG, a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Kenneth Cassel, has raised $5M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of nine private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and one public comparable, though peer-median methodology could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints within the Robotics & Manufacturing sector.

Stage-progression analysis, the sole applicable triangulation method, applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier to the $5M raised proxy, reflecting typical Seed-to-next-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $12M. The lack of sufficiently narrow peer clustering in the robotics sector prevented median-based cross-checks, limiting the analysis to single-method triangulation.

The quantitative range supported by this analysis is $11M to $14M, with a median point of $12M. Key uncertainties stem from the narrow methodological foundation: the absence of comparable seed-stage companies in robotics limits ability to validate the 2.5x multiplier against peer norms, and reliance on stage-progression alone leaves the valuation sensitive to assumptions about RMFG's competitive positioning and capital efficiency relative to sector peers. Additional clarity on unit economics and market traction would strengthen conviction in this range.
